steps.title: Start with the per-acre range
steps.content:
The estimate is at least $40$ and no more than $55$ spruce trees per acre. That gives the per-acre inequality:
$$40\leq \text{trees per acre}\leq 55$$

steps.title: Scale to four acres
steps.content:
The question asks about a $4$-acre section, so multiply both bounds by $4$:
$$40(4)\leq x\leq 55(4)$$
$$160\leq x\leq 220$$

steps.title: Compare the bounds
steps.content: Choice A leaves the estimate per acre. Choice C scales only the upper bound. Choice D has the lower bound greater than the upper bound. The interval $160\leq x\leq 220$ represents the $4$-acre estimate. The answer is B.
